tasks.title: Scaffold Ground Control CLI/TUI with Go + Bubble Tea
tasks.description: Initialize the Go project structure for Ground Control.

tasks.title: Investigate and resolve 'gc' command name conflict
tasks.description: Resolved by remapping git aliases to gt* prefix.

tasks.title: Implement standup command with Taskmaster integration
tasks.description: Daily standup ritual command.

tasks.title: Expand TUI with task detail view and keyboard navigation
tasks.description: Enhanced TUI with detail view, state changes, help.

tasks.title: Implement verification system for task completion
tasks.description: Verification before marking tasks done.

tasks.title: Design Ground Control command surface and workflow
tasks.description: Holistic design of the remaining CLI commands and their interactions. This planning task covers: gc create, gc process, gc taskmaster, gc start, gc orchestrate, and how they work together with the TUI.

context.background: Ground Control has basic CLI infrastructure (tasks, dump, complete, standup, tui). Now we need to design the remaining commands that complete the workflow: task creation, brain dump processing, taskmaster orchestration, and Claude Code integration (single + parallel execution). These commands are interconnected and should be designed together.
requirements: Design gc create — interactive task creation
Design gc process — brain dump → task conversion
Design gc taskmaster — state review and recommendations
Design gc start — single task → Claude Code handoff
Design gc orchestrate — multi-task parallel execution
Design gc tasks improvements — indexes, hide completed, filters
Design TUI enhancements — multi-select mode for orchestrate
Design config system — max_parallel_agents, default settings
Design Claude Code integration pattern (works for start AND orchestrate)
Define the complete user journey from idea → execution
Ensure consistency across CLI and TUI interfaces
constraints: Use Claude Code CLI (not SDK) to leverage subscription
Keep commands composable and Unix-philosophy aligned
TUI and CLI should offer equivalent functionality
Design for extensibility (future: projects, weekly ritual, ai-matt)

topics: What's the ideal user journey from brain dump to completed task?
How should gc start and gc orchestrate share Claude Code integration?
What's the right abstraction for parallel task execution?
How do we handle task dependencies in orchestration?
What config options are needed and where do they live?

tasks.title: Enhance gc tasks with indexes, filtering, and hide completed
tasks.description: Upgrade the gc tasks command to show dynamic indexes (1-based, assigned at display time), filter by state/tag/project, and hide completed tasks by default.

context.background: Current gc tasks shows all tasks without indexes. Per docs/command-design.md, we need dynamic indexes for use with gc orc, filtering options, and completed tasks hidden by default.
requirements: Add 1-based dynamic indexes to task list output
Hide completed tasks by default (add --all flag to show them)
Add --state=<state> filter
Add --tag=<tag> filter
Add --project=<project> filter
Add --json flag for scripting
Update output format per design doc
constraints: Indexes are ephemeral, recalculated each run
Maintain backward compatibility with existing output for scripts

tasks.title: Create Context Manager agent and context bundle system
tasks.description: Define the Context Manager agent and implement the context bundle creation system. This agent is called by Taskmaster during task creation to build comprehensive context bundles.

context.background: Per docs/command-design.md, every task needs a context bundle built at creation time. The Context Manager agent gathers relevant code snippets, project patterns, past decisions, and conversation context.
requirements: Create agents/context-manager.md with agent prompt
Add context_bundle field to Task type
Implement context bundle directory structure (task_<id>/context/)
Create requirements.md generator from task description
Create relevant_code extractor (find affected files, extract snippets)
Create patterns.md extractor from project docs
Create decisions.md extractor from activity-log.json
Create test_hints.md generator
Integrate with Taskmaster flow
constraints: Extract code snippets, not full files
Decisions should be relevant to the task, not all decisions
Context bundle should be self-contained for agent consumption

tasks.title: Implement gc create command with Context Manager integration
tasks.description: Interactive task creation command that guides users through task creation and calls Context Manager to build the context bundle.

context.background: Per docs/command-design.md, gc create is an interactive command for task creation. It collects task details and calls Context Manager to build the context bundle.
requirements: Implement interactive prompts: title, description, type, complexity, importance, tags, project, blocked_by, verification
Add --quick flag for minimal prompts (title + type)
Call Context Manager after task skeleton is created
Save task with context bundle to tasks.json
Log task creation to activity-log.json
constraints: Use bubbletea or simple stdin prompts for interactivity
Provide sensible defaults where possible
Validate inputs before saving

tasks.title: Implement gc process command for brain dump conversion
tasks.description: Command to process brain dump entries into tasks using Taskmaster analysis and Context Manager for context bundles.

context.background: Per docs/command-design.md, gc process converts brain dump entries to tasks. Taskmaster analyzes entries to determine task type, complexity, and requirements, then calls Context Manager to build context bundles.
requirements: Process all unprocessed entries from brain-dump.json
Add --dry-run flag to show what would be created
Add --interactive flag to approve each conversion
Analyze entry content for task type signals
Extract urgency hints
Identify related existing tasks
Call Context Manager for each created task
Update brain-dump.json with processed status and converted_to
constraints: Should work without Claude Code for now (rule-based analysis)
Later: integrate with actual Taskmaster agent via Claude Code

tasks.title: Implement gc orc command with session management
tasks.description: Create the gc orc command that orchestrates task execution. This is the entry point that accepts task indexes/IDs, creates a work session, and kicks off the pipeline.

context.background: Per docs/command-design.md, gc orc is the core orchestration command. It accepts task indexes (from gc tasks) or task IDs, creates a work session for tracking, and executes tasks through the pipeline.
requirements: Accept task indexes (gc orc 1 3 5) or task IDs
Resolve indexes to task IDs using current gc tasks output
Create work session in data/sessions/session_<timestamp>.json
Add --dry-run flag to show execution plan without running
Add --verbose flag for detailed output
Track session state (started, running, completed, failed)
Wire up to pipeline executor (task_1741273000002)
constraints: Single task execution for Phase 3 (parallel comes in Phase 5)
Session must persist across potential crashes for resumption

tasks.title: Create pipeline executor with stage framework
tasks.description: Build the pipeline executor that runs tasks through stages: Sanity Check → Coder → Reviewer → Tester → Repo Update. Each stage has a defined interface.

context.background: The pipeline executor manages stage transitions, handles feedback loops, and tracks progress. Each stage implements a common interface for execution.
requirements: Define Stage interface: Execute(task, context) -> StageResult
StageResult contains: status (success/needs_revision/escalate), output files, issues[]
Implement pipeline runner that executes stages in order
Handle stage transitions and state updates
Implement Sanity Check stage (verify deps met, context exists)
Track stage outputs in session
Support stage skipping via config
constraints: Stages must be independently testable
Pipeline state must be recoverable for --continue support

tasks.title: Implement Coder stage with Claude Code CLI integration
tasks.description: Create the Coder stage that invokes Claude Code CLI to implement task requirements using the context bundle.

context.background: The Coder stage uses Claude Code CLI (not SDK) to implement code changes. It passes the context bundle and receives implementation output.
requirements: Implement Coder stage following Stage interface
Build Claude Code CLI invocation with context bundle
Pass requirements.md, patterns.md, relevant_code as context
Capture implementation output and changed files
Generate implementation_notes.md from Claude output
Handle Claude Code CLI errors gracefully
Support --verbose flag for streaming output
constraints: Use claude CLI command, not SDK API
Respect config timeout settings
Must work in git worktree (preparation for Phase 5)

tasks.title: Implement Code Reviewer stage with feedback generation
tasks.description: Create the Code Reviewer stage that reviews Coder output and generates structured feedback for revision loops.

context.background: The Reviewer stage examines code changes against requirements and patterns, producing either approval or specific feedback for the Coder to address.
requirements: Implement Reviewer stage following Stage interface
Invoke Claude Code CLI with review prompt
Pass code changes, requirements, patterns as context
Generate structured review_feedback.md
Return decision: approved | needs_revision | escalate
Track review iteration count
Include issues[] in output for self-learning
constraints: Review prompt must produce actionable feedback
Must not approve code that doesn't meet requirements

tasks.title: Implement feedback loop and escalation handling
tasks.description: Add feedback loop logic between Coder and Reviewer stages with escalation to human after max iterations.

context.background: Per docs/command-design.md, feedback loops allow revision cycles between stages. After 3 failed iterations, the system escalates to human decision.
requirements: Track iteration count per feedback loop
Pass review feedback to Coder on needs_revision
Implement max iteration check (default 3, configurable)
On escalation: set task state to waiting
On escalation: generate escalation summary with all attempts
Implement gc orc --continue to resume after human input
Log all iterations for self-learning system
constraints: Must preserve full feedback history for human review
Escalation should not lose work done so far

tasks.title: Implement Tester stage with test execution
tasks.description: Create the Tester stage that runs existing tests, writes new tests based on test_hints.md, and provides feedback for test failures.

context.background: The Tester stage runs after code review approval. It executes existing tests, can write new tests based on test_hints.md, and triggers a feedback loop with Coder if tests fail.
requirements: Implement Tester stage following Stage interface
Run existing test suite (go test ./...)
Parse test output for pass/fail status
Generate test_results.md with summary
Support feedback loop to Coder on test failures
Use test_hints.md from context bundle for guidance
Include issues[] for self-learning on test failures
constraints: Must handle different test frameworks (go test for now)
Test failures should produce actionable feedback for Coder

tasks.title: Implement Repo Update stage (docs + commit + deploy)
tasks.description: Create the combined Repo Update stage that updates documentation, creates commits, and optionally deploys.

context.background: The Repo Update stage is the final pipeline stage. It updates affected documentation, creates a git commit with proper message, and optionally triggers deployment.
requirements: Implement RepoUpdate stage following Stage interface
Update inline comments and docs based on changes
Generate commit message from implementation_notes.md
Stage and commit changes with proper message
Run pre-commit hooks and handle failures
Support optional deployment trigger (if task.deploy_target set)
Include Co-Authored-By for AI attribution
constraints: Never force push or destructive git operations
Commit message should follow project conventions
Deployment is optional and configurable

tasks.title: Implement Taskmaster session review step
tasks.description: Add end-of-session review by Taskmaster that validates agent-reported issues and identifies systemic patterns for self-learning.

context.background: Per docs/command-design.md, after each work session completes, Taskmaster performs a deep review of all issues reported by agents, validates they are real problems, and logs them for self-learning.
requirements: Add session review step after all tasks complete in gc orc
Collect all issues from session
Validate issues are real (not box-filling)
Identify systemic issues across the session
Log validated issues to data/learning-log.json
Generate session summary with issue counts
constraints: Review should happen automatically at session end
Must distinguish real issues from noise

tasks.title: Wire up complete pipeline in gc orc
tasks.description: Integrate all pipeline stages into gc orc command with proper stage transitions and configuration.

context.background: With all stages implemented, wire them together in gc orc so it runs the full pipeline: Sanity → Coder → Reviewer → Tester → Repo Update → Session Review.
requirements: Update gc orc to use pipeline executor with all stages
Add Coder and Reviewer stages with feedback loop
Add Tester stage with test feedback loop
Add Repo Update stage
Add Session Review at end
Support --skip-review, --skip-test flags
Update task state throughout pipeline
constraints: Maintain backward compatibility with existing gc orc flags
Pipeline should be configurable via data/config.json

tasks.title: Design and run manual integration tests for Ground Control
tasks.description: Create a manual test plan to validate the end-to-end flow of Ground Control. This should verify that all commands work correctly and that the overall UX is coherent and intuitive. The goal is to experience the system as a user would - from brain dump to task completion.

context.background: We've implemented the core Ground Control pipeline: brain dump processing, task creation, and the orchestration pipeline (Sanity → Coder → Reviewer → Tester → Repo Update). Now we need to validate it all works together and provides a good user experience.
requirements: Test gc dump - can capture ideas quickly
Test gc process - converts brain dumps to tasks with context bundles
Test gc create - interactive task creation works
Test gc tasks - shows tasks with proper filtering and indexes
Test gc orc - runs pipeline stages correctly
Test gc orc --continue - resumes paused sessions
Test gc orc --dry-run - shows plan without executing
Verify error messages are helpful and actionable
Verify the flow from idea to completion feels natural
Document any UX friction points or confusing behaviors
constraints: Focus on user experience, not just functionality
Test with realistic scenarios, not contrived examples

outputs.description: Test plan with scenarios and expected outcomes
Results from running the tests with observations

suggested_next_steps: Run through each command in sequence
Fix critical issues before broader testing

tasks.title: Implement gc self-learn command
tasks.description: Build the self-learning command that analyzes learning-log.json, identifies patterns in issues, and proposes improvements. This completes the learning loop - we already collect issues during gc orc, but have no way to analyze or act on them.

context.background: The self-learning system is designed in docs/command-design.md (lines 820-1243). Infrastructure exists: SessionIssue struct, session_review.go writes to learning-log.json, issues are collected during gc orc. What's missing is the command to analyze and propose improvements.
requirements: Create internal/cmd/selflearn.go with gc self-learn command
Load and parse learning-log.json
Group issues by category and identify frequency patterns
Generate human-readable proposals for improvements
Support --apply flag to apply approved changes
Track applied improvements in data/improvements-applied.json
constraints: Follow design in docs/command-design.md
Proposals must be specific and actionable
Human approval required before applying changes

tasks.title: Enhance TUI with orchestration progress and task creation
tasks.description: Add missing TUI features: orchestration progress view to watch pipeline execution, and task creation flow without leaving TUI.

context.background: TUI exists (internal/tui/tui.go) with list, detail, state-change, and help views. Planned but not implemented: multi-select, orchestration progress, brain dump processing, task creation.
requirements: Add orchestration progress view - watch gc orc pipeline stages in real-time
Add task creation view - interactive form to create tasks without leaving TUI
Consider brain dump quick-capture from TUI
constraints: Maintain existing TUI patterns and styling
Keep keyboard navigation consistent

tasks.title: Add task type routing to gc orc pipeline
tasks.description: Currently gc orc runs the same coding pipeline (Sanity → Coder → Reviewer → Tester → Repo Update) for ALL task types. This is incorrect - planning tasks, research tasks, and human-input tasks should have different workflows. Add type-based routing to select appropriate pipeline.

context.background: Task types are defined in types.go (simple, ai-planning, research, coding, human-input) but gc orc ignores them. The CLAUDE.md agent roster suggests different agents for different task types (planner, researcher, coder) but this routing doesn't exist.
requirements: Check task.Type before selecting pipeline
coding tasks: current pipeline (Sanity → Coder → Reviewer → Tester → Repo Update)
ai-planning tasks: Sanity → Planner → Human Review
research tasks: Sanity → Researcher → Summary
human-input tasks: Notify and wait, no automated pipeline
simple tasks: Quick execution, maybe skip review/test?
Warn user if task type doesn't match available pipeline
constraints: Don't break existing coding pipeline
Gracefully handle missing agent implementations

tasks.title: Implement proper permission handling for Claude CLI
tasks.description: Replace --dangerously-skip-permissions with proper permission mode handling. Consider pre-granting permissions or using --permission-mode with appropriate settings for headless execution.

context.background: Currently using --dangerously-skip-permissions for headless Claude CLI execution. This works but bypasses all safety checks. Should implement proper permission handling using --permission-mode or pre-granted permissions.
requirements: Research Claude CLI permission modes (default, acceptEdits, bypassPermissions, plan, etc.)
Determine appropriate mode for automated pipeline execution
Consider allowing configuration of permission mode
Update internal/claude/claude.go to use proper permissions
Document security implications
constraints: Must work in headless/non-interactive mode
Should not require manual permission grants during execution

tasks.title: Add Sprint entity to Ground Control
tasks.description: Add Sprint entity to Ground Control. Sprint is a lightweight grouping of related tasks toward a goal or work session. Schema: id, name, description, goal, tasks[] (task IDs), status (active/completed/paused), created_at, completed_at. Add to data/sprints.json. Commands: gc sprint create, gc sprint list, gc sprint add-task <sprint> <task>, gc sprint status. Keep it simple - not full project management, just work session grouping. Should be able to run gc orc on a sprint to execute all its tasks.

Filters by name case-insensitive
Optional: filter by channel_type

tasks.title: 5.2 Notification Cards in Focused Mode

swipeEnabled={true}
Actions: Archive, Mark Important

tasks.title: 5.3 Auto-Read Implementation
tasks.description: Copy IntersectionObserver pattern from CleanView.tsx. Add tier-adjustment formula. Only activate when Stage 3 is visible. Note: ai_tier field may not exist - use 0 as fallback. Check localStorage for notifai-auto-read-delay setting (default 5000ms).

context.background: NotifAI Carousel UI - Phase 5. Auto-read marks notifications as read after time in viewport. Tier-adjusted means important notifications get more reading time.
requirements: Base time from settings (3s/5s/10s/15s)
+3s if tier == CRITICAL
+2s if tier == HIGH
+2s if ai_tier == 1
+1s if ai_tier == 2
-2s if is_user_important == true
-1s if ai_tier == 4-5
Minimum 2s
Use IntersectionObserver
Only active in Focused stage
constraints: Must not trigger in other stages
Existing IntersectionObserver logic may exist to reference

tasks.title: 5.4 Empty State for Focused Feed
tasks.description: Simple centered message: All caught up, No unread notifications. Add Adjust filters button that calls goToStage(1). Do not overcomplicate.

context.background: NotifAI Carousel UI - Phase 5. Empty state provides positive feedback when inbox is clear.
requirements: Friendly empty message
"All caught up" or similar
Link to adjust filters if too aggressive
constraints: Should feel encouraging, not broken

tasks.title: 5.5 Peek Integration for Focused Stage
